name: Dash Runner

notes.en: Swipe left/right on the trail (or the ◀/▶ buttons) to change lanes; swipe up (or ▲) to jump, swipe down (or ▼) to duck. Arrow keys / WASD work too — Left/Right change lanes, Up jumps, Down ducks.
notes.ru: Свайпай влево/вправо по тропе (или кнопки ◀/▶), чтобы сменить полосу; свайп вверх (или ▲) — прыжок, свайп вниз (или ▼) — подкат. Стрелки / WASD тоже работают: влево/вправо — смена полосы, вверх — прыжок, вниз — подкат.

notes: Pseudo-3D perspective renderer (render.js), reusing cross-the-road's exact scale = FOCAL/(FOCAL+depth) projection: the trail narrows toward a horizon, sprites/obstacles scale by depth, camera rides with the player. Auto-runs forward at an ever-increasing speed — speed is a pure function of distance (track.js's speedForDistance()), ramping from 5 to 11 world-units/s over the first 260 units then holding (endless). 3 lanes, swipe/tap/keys to change lanes (smooth tween), jump (real gravity arc) over low obstacles, duck (timed slide) under overhead obstacles, or dodge lanes around full-height pillars — collision unifies all three via one interval-overlap test across lane/depth/vertical axes. Obstacle generation guarantees by construction that at most 2 of 3 lanes are ever blocked at a segment, and paces segments by a time-based (not just distance-based) gap floor so a worst-case edge-to-edge lane change always has time to complete — see track.js's getFairnessReport() and the README's Fairness section. No bundled image or audio assets — canvas vector shapes with a neon glow, sound reuses games/_shared/js/ogh-sfx.js (extended with one new 'duck' pattern). Best distance persists via OGHProfile, same convention as games/cross-the-road and games/pop-the-bugs.
